assertions.description: Output is inline HTML — no markdown
assertions.check: response contains <style> and <div or <table but zero markdown table syntax (|---|)

assertions.description: Both entities classified as companies, no sector entity
assertions.check: no sector summary cards present; quarter card blocks shown for NVDA and AMD

assertions.description: 9 scoring dimensions in scorecard
assertions.check: scorecard contains 9 data rows before the Overall Wins row — including Earnings Reaction and Financial Outlook rows

assertions.description: 2-company color rule: winner green + loser red
assertions.check: winner cells use #dcfce7 / #15803d; loser cells use #fee2e2 / #b91c1c

assertions.description: Quarter cards shown for both companies
assertions.check: two company-block elements with qtr-card elements; no sector summary cards

assertions.description: Section 4 Financial Outlook present
assertions.check: revenue, EPS, EBITDA, FCF table present — not omitted

assertions.description: Chart.js loaded exactly once
assertions.check: cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js appears exactly one time

assertions.description: Verdict names overall leader with bottom-line pick
assertions.check: Section 8 contains 'Overall leader:' and 'If you had to pick one'

assertions.description: getAnalytics called per quarter — 8 calls total
assertions.check: getAnalytics invoked 4 times for NVDA and 4 times for AMD with individual documentIDs

name: Sector vs Sector — tech vs healthcare
input: Compare the tech sector vs healthcare

mcp_batches.batch_1: no API calls — sectors normalized only

assertions.description: Sector names normalized correctly
assertions.check: 'tech' mapped to 'Information Technology'; 'healthcare' mapped to 'Health Care' in API calls

assertions.description: No getCompanyDescription calls in Batch 1
assertions.check: Batch 1 makes zero API calls — sectors require no description lookup

assertions.description: 7 scoring dimensions — no company-only rows
assertions.check: scorecard contains 7 data rows; no Earnings Reaction row; no Financial Outlook row; no CEO/CFO rows

assertions.description: Section 4 Financial Outlook OMITTED
assertions.check: no Revenue / EPS / EBITDA / FCF table present in response

assertions.description: Sector summary cards shown — not quarter cards
assertions.check: Section 2 shows sector-summary-grid elements for both sectors; no qtr-card elements

assertions.description: Each sector card shows: sentiment score, investment score, top mover, fastest-rising theme, dominant events
assertions.check: sector summary cards contain at least: sentiment score, investment score, top mover name and %, fastest-rising topic name and %

assertions.description: Entity type labeled in headers
assertions.check: section headers or column headers contain '(Sector)' label for both entities

assertions.description: Sectors use array format in all API calls
assertions.check: sectors param always array: sectors: ['Information Technology'] — never a string

assertions.description: 2-entity color rule applied
assertions.check: winner entity uses #dcfce7 (green), loser entity uses #fee2e2 (red) in scorecard

assertions.description: Verdict acknowledges no financial forecasts at sector level
assertions.check: Verdict or footnote notes financial outlook unavailable for sector comparisons

name: Company vs Sector — NVDA vs tech sector
input: How does NVDA compare to the tech sector?

assertions.description: NVDA classified as company, tech sector classified as sector
assertions.check: NVDA shows quarter cards; IT shows sector summary card; both labeled with type

assertions.description: 7 universal scoring rows + 2 company-only rows with N/A for sector
assertions.check: Earnings Reaction row shows NVDA score and 'N/A — Sector' for IT column; Financial Outlook row same

assertions.description: Sector column in Financial Outlook shows N/A — not empty
assertions.check: IT sector column in Section 4 table shows 'N/A — Sector' in all metric rows

assertions.description: Verdict includes mixed comparison context paragraph
assertions.check: Verdict contains language about single-stock concentration vs sector breadth; acknowledges not apples-to-apples

assertions.description: Stock comparison footnoted for sector
assertions.check: IT sector stock value is footnoted as top-mover proxy (e.g. 'NVDA — sector leader')

assertions.description: Only NVDA uses getCompanyDescription in Batch 1
assertions.check: getCompanyDescription called once (for NVDA); no description call for 'Information Technology'

assertions.description: Entity types labeled in column headers
assertions.check: scorecard column header for NVDA shows '(Company)'; IT column shows '(Sector)'

name: Three-entity comparison — two companies vs sector
input: Compare NVDA and AMD against the semiconductor sector

assertions.description: Sub-sector normalized correctly
assertions.check: 'semiconductor sector' mapped to 'Information Technology-Semiconductors and Semiconductor Equipment'

assertions.description: Three entity columns in scorecard
assertions.check: scorecard contains NVDA column, AMD column, and Semiconductors (Sector) column

assertions.description: Three-company rule: only winner gets green, no red losers
assertions.check: with 3 entities, only winner cells use #dcfce7; no #fee2e2 loser cells

assertions.description: Sector entity column shows N/A in company-only rows
assertions.check: Earnings Reaction and Financial Outlook rows show 'N/A — Sector' in Semiconductors sector column

assertions.description: Three colors assigned correctly
assertions.check: Entity A (NVDA) = #3B82F6, Entity B (AMD) = #8B5CF6, Entity C (Semiconductors Sector) = #F59E0B

name: Skill should NOT trigger — single sector
